observations.value: POST /session with completely invalid credentials (e.g., identifier=wrong@wrong.com, secret=wrongpassword) returns HTTP 200 and sets a valid session cookie (Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/), instead of returning 401 Unauthorized. The login endpoint accepts any non-empty username/password combination as valid, bypassing all credential verification. Only empty fields trigger the correct 401 'Invalid credentials.' response.

POST /session with identifier=admin@example.com&secret=password returns HTTP 200 and Set-Cookie: session=ok — this may be a valid test account, but combined with the above, the server appears to authenticate any non-empty credentials, not just known accounts.

POST /session with identifier=user@example.com&secret=password also returns HTTP 200 and Set-Cookie: session=ok, further confirming that credential validation is not being enforced — any non-empty identifier/secret pair is accepted.

detections.observation_text: POST /session with completely invalid credentials (e.g., identifier=wrong@wrong.com, secret=wrongpassword) returns HTTP 200 and sets a valid session cookie (Set-Cookie: session=ok; Path=/), instead of returning 401 Unauthorized. The login endpoint accepts any non-empty username/password combination as valid, bypassing all credential verification. Only empty fields trigger the correct 401 'Invalid credentials.' response.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a credential-bypass bug (any non-empty credentials succeed) that is not present in the manifest, and while it would be a real security issue, no manifest entry covers this behavior; the closest entry (t1_login_500) expects a 500 error on login, not a spurious 200 with a cookie.

detections.observation_text: POST /session with identifier=admin@example.com&secret=password returns HTTP 200 and Set-Cookie: session=ok — this may be a valid test account, but combined with the above, the server appears to authenticate any non-empty credentials, not just known accounts.

judge_verdict.label: noise
judge_verdict.reason: The observation is speculative ('appears to authenticate any non-empty credentials') and does not describe a concrete, specific regression matching any manifest entry; it notes a 200 response on login, which is the expected success behavior, not a 500 error or other documented bug.

detections.observation_text: POST /session with identifier=user@example.com&secret=password also returns HTTP 200 and Set-Cookie: session=ok, further confirming that credential validation is not being enforced — any non-empty identifier/secret pair is accepted.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a weak credential-validation policy (any non-empty credentials accepted), which is not listed in the manifest and is not covered by the testapp's public rules as a planted regression; the login returns 200 with a valid session cookie rather than the 500 error described in t1_login_500.
